User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the MBN school council application, including their profile information, role, and experience points.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for authentication and identification. This is a unique identifier provided by the external authentication system (Firebase Google Sign-in).

displayName.description: The user's display name, typically obtained from the authentication provider.

role.description: The role assigned to the user within the application, determining their access rights.

xp.description: Experience points earned by the user, initially 100 for new student sign-ins.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Proposal.title: Proposal
Proposal.description: Represents an open proposal or announcement managed by administrators or council members, with adjustable visibility settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Proposal entity.

title.description: The title or subject of the proposal.

content.description: The detailed content or description of the proposal.

authorId.description: Reference to the User who created this proposal. (Relationship: User 1:N Proposal)

visibility.description: Defines which user roles can view this proposal.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the proposal was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the proposal was last updated.

MeetingMinute.title: MeetingMinute
MeetingMinute.description: Represents the official minutes of a meeting, accessible to administrators and council members.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MeetingMinute entity.

title.description: The title or subject of the meeting minutes.

content.description: The detailed content of the meeting minutes, including discussions and decisions.

meetingDate.description: The date when the meeting took place.

authorId.description: Reference to the User (Administrator or Council member) who recorded or published these meeting minutes. (Relationship: User 1:N MeetingMinute)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the meeting minutes record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the meeting minutes record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes the user's `role` (e.g., 'Student', 'Teacher') and `xp` points. Read/write access is typically for the user themselves, with Admin/Council having broader management rights.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for 'Admin' roles. The existence of a document at this path signifies that the `userId` has 'Admin' privileges. This enables highly efficient and atomic authorization checks (`exists(...)`) in security rules. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds the 'Admin' role.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for 'Council' roles. The existence of a document at this path signifies that the `userId` has 'Council' privileges. This enables highly efficient and atomic authorization checks (`exists(...)`) in security rules. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds the 'Council' role.

definition.description: Stores open proposals or announcements. Access to individual proposals is controlled by the `visibility` field within the document (e.g., 'STUDENT', 'TEACHER', 'COUNCIL', 'ADMIN_ONLY'), ensuring Authorization Independence. Only Admin and Council members can create, edit, or remove proposals.

params.name: proposalId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific proposal.

definition.description: Stores official meeting minutes. Access is strictly limited to users with 'Admin' or 'Council' roles, enforced by security rules checking `roles_admin` and `roles_council` collections. This ensures a homogeneous security posture for the entire collection.

params.name: meetingMinuteId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific meeting minutes record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Role Denormalization:** For critical roles like 'Admin' and 'Council', separate top-level collections (`/roles_admin/{userId}` and `/roles_council/{userId}`) are used. This denormalization means that security rules can check `exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` instead of `get(/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'Admin'`. This completely eliminates `get()` calls for these primary authorization checks, which is crucial for performance, atomic operations, and rule debuggability. This pattern makes authorization explicit and independent of other document fields or hierarchical data.
2.  **Self-Contained Proposal Visibility:** For `Proposal` documents, the `visibility` field is stored directly within each `proposal` document. This allows security rules to evaluate access rights (e.g., `resource.data.visibility == 'STUDENT'`) without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents or other collections. This ensures that the access decision for a proposal is atomic and contained within the document itself.
3.  **User Profile Roles:** For 'Teacher' and 'Student' roles, the `role` field within `/users/{userId}` is used. While this requires a `get(/users/$(request.auth.uid))` call, it is a lookup on the requesting user's own profile, which is generally acceptable for user-specific attributes and avoids complex cross-document dependencies.

**Querying and Authorization Posture (QAPs):**
1.  **Proposals:** The `/proposals` collection has a homogeneous security posture in terms of document type, but access to individual documents is governed by their `visibility` field. This enables secure `list` operations. Clients can query `/proposals` and filter by specific visibility states, and security rules will enforce that only documents matching the user's role and the `visibility` setting are returned (e.g., a student can only see proposals with `visibility: 'STUDENT'` or `visibility: 'ALL'`). This adheres to QAP as rules will prevent unauthorized reads of proposals with higher visibility, acting as an enforcement mechanism, not a filter.
2.  **Meeting Minutes:** The `/meetingMinutes` collection is segregated to contain only meeting minutes, which have a very strict, homogeneous security posture: only 'Admin' and 'Council' members can read, create, update, or delete them. This allows for simple and robust security rules based solely on the `isAdmin()` or `isCouncil()` checks, ensuring that `list` operations on this collection are inherently secure without requiring complex document-level filtering.
3.  **User Profiles:** The `/users` collection also maintains a homogeneous security posture where users can manage their own profiles, and 'Admin' and 'Council' roles have broader read/write access. `list` operations for users can be restricted to 'Admin' or 'Council' based on their respective role checks.

**Overall Strategy Adherence:**
*   **Structural Segregation:** Each collection (`/users`, `/roles_admin`, `/roles_council`, `/proposals`, `/meetingMinutes`) serves a distinct purpose and has a consistent security posture, simplifying rule definitions.
*   **Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership is used for user profiles (`/users/{userId}`). Global roles are implemented using existence checks in dedicated collections (`/roles_admin`, `/roles_council`). Collaborative-style access for proposals is managed via an explicit `visibility` field.
*   **Data Clarity:** Fields like `visibility` explicitly define access states, and consistent naming conventions are used throughout (e.g., `userId`, `proposalId`, `authorId`).
